message:
feat(browser): add host-ready notification to browser reactor and edge bridge types to Next.js SDK

Browser reactor gains notify_ready() — the key integration point for browser
host bridges (fetch completion, WebSocket events, stream callbacks) to deliver
token readiness into the reactor event queue. The method:
- Validates the token exists and the readiness intersects registered interest
- Coalesces with existing pending events when coalesce_wakes is enabled
- Sets wake_pending atomically so the next poll picks up the events

Extracted readiness_mask() as a shared helper replacing inline Interest
flag unions. The reregister path now filters pending events to remove
stale readiness bits that no longer match the updated interest mask.

wake() rewritten to snapshot readiness events and merge them into the
pending queue atomically, fixing a race where wake_pending could be set
without any events actually being queued.

Next.js SDK (@asupersync/next) adds full edge bridge type surface:
- NextEdgeBridgeEnvironment, NextEdgeBridgeDiagnostics,
  NextEdgeBridgeRequestOptions, NextEdgeBridgeRequest,
  NextEdgeBridgeResponse, NextEdgeBridgeAdapterOptions,
  NextEdgeBridgeRuntimeError types
- createNextEdgeBridgeDiagnostics() factory function
- createNextEdgeBridgeAdapter() for constructing edge-safe bridge adapters
  that explicitly disable direct runtime execution
- createNextBridgeLogFields() broadened to accept edge diagnostics
- NEXT_EDGE_BRIDGE_ENVIRONMENTS constant

Minor fixes across other reactor backends (epoll, kqueue, macos, windows,
source) for consistency and formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
